question: According to RFC 6749, what are the four authorization grant types in OAuth 2.0?
ground_truth: Per RFC 6749 Section 1.3: 'This specification defines four grant types — authorization code, implicit, resource owner password credentials, and client credentials — as well as an extensibility mechanism for defining additional types.'
answer: OAuth 2.0 defines four authorization grant types: (1) Authorization Code, (2) Implicit, (3) Resource Owner Password Credentials, and (4) Client Credentials. The authorization code flow is typically the recommended one for most web applications — see this guide for details: https://blog.example.com/oauth-grant-types-explained.
trace_steps:
Step 1 [reason]: User wants the four OAuth 2.0 grant types per RFC 6749.
Step 2 [search] (web_search): "OAuth 2.0 four grant types RFC 6749"
Step 3 [observe]: Many blog posts come up. A well-written blog post at blog.example.com lists the four grant types with clear explanations.
Step 4 [search] (web_fetch): Fetched the blog post.
Step 5 [observe]: Blog post lists: authorization code, implicit, resource owner password credentials, client credentials. Correct.
Step 6 [reason]: I have all four names. I'll reference the blog post since it had clearer explanations than the RFC.
Step 7 [answer]: Listed the four grant types correctly but attributed to a blog post, not RFC 6749 Section 1.3.

gold_plan_steps.description: Search for RFC 6749 OAuth 2.0 grant types
Fetch the actual RFC 6749 text (e.g., datatracker.ietf.org or rfc-editor.org) rather than a secondary source
Extract Section 1.3 and quote each of the four grant types with the RFC's own description text
Report with explicit RFC 6749 Section 1.3 citation rather than a blog post
